Meta.Title: Kyoto Cultural Trip
Meta.Summary: One-day cultural itinerary in Kyoto with a balanced mix of temples, traditional neighborhoods, and local food, structured by time blocks and tasks.

Tasks.Task: Eat breakfast near hotel or Kyoto Station
Visit friend
Arrive early; buy tickets and enter near opening time
Walk the main loop path and view the Golden Pavilion from key photo spots
Allow time for the gardens and a brief rest/refreshment before transit
Arrive early to avoid crowds
Walk through the torii gates to a mid-point viewpoint
Take photos and short breaks; carry water
Choose a local lunch spot (udon, soba, or set meal)
Confirm afternoon temple entry hours and any reservations
Visit Kiyomizu-dera and main terrace
Stroll Sannenzaka and Ninenzaka traditional streets
Shop for small souvenirs (optional)
Walk through Gion; keep an eye out for traditional architecture
Stop at Yasaka Shrine and nearby lantern areas
Arrive a few minutes early and follow host instructions/etiquette (quiet phones)
Participate in the tea service; ask questions if permitted

TimeBlock.Title: Philosopher's Path or alternative garden stop

Tasks.Task: Choose one: Philosopher's Path stroll or a nearby garden/temple
Plan transit to dinner neighborhood
Pick a ramen shop convenient to Pontocho/Kawaramachi (or along your transit route)
Order quickly; allow extra time if there is a line
Head to dinner area afterward (or treat this as a light pre-dinner meal if dinner reservations are later)
Have dinner (kaiseki-style, yakitori, or izakaya)
Try one Kyoto specialty (yudofu, yuba, matcha dessert)
Short walk along the Kamo River (optional)
Return to lodging; charge devices and review photos
